---
title: "Design"
subtitle: "Architecture notes, ADRs, and audits"
---

# What lives here

This section is the *why* behind the code. If the [Guide](../guide/app-and-document.qmd)
tells you how to use hwpapi, the Design section tells you how it got
to be shaped this way.

# Architecture

- [Architecture overview](architecture.qmd) — the v2 import tree,
  the two-layer model, the collection protocol, and how they fit
  together.

# Architecture Decision Records

Each ADR documents one accepted decision, its context, alternatives,
and consequences. They are the primary historical record for v2
design choices.

- [ADR-001: Two-layer API (`hwpapi.low` + `App.doc.*`)](adr/adr-001-v2-redesign.qmd)
- [ADR-002: Quarto single-pipeline documentation](adr/adr-002-quarto-docs.qmd)

# Audits and design notes (archived)

These pre-v2 documents led to the redesign. They are kept for
provenance — the authoritative current spec is the ADRs plus the
live codebase.

- [`baseline-v1.0.md`](../design/baseline-v1.0.md) — v1 performance baseline
- [`public-api-v1.0.txt`](../design/public-api-v1.0.txt) — v1 public symbol dump
- [`app-public-v2.txt`](../design/app-public-v2.txt) — v2 `dir(App)` snapshot
- [`app-member-audit.md`](../design/app-member-audit.md) — the 82-member disposition matrix
